# P1 Workflow Architecture Plan
# Generated by planner agent — source of truth for builder implementation
# DO NOT EDIT — update via planner agent if changes are needed

## Task: P1 Workflow Architecture — F-14, F-04, F-05, F-07

---

## Scope

**In:**
- F-14 (Persistent Memory / Project Picture) — schema additions, Update Project Picture sub-workflow (Workflow 09), `project_picture` key format
- F-04 (Standup Transcript Ingestion) — Drive polling, text/audio parsing, attribution, deduplication (Workflow 10)
- F-05 (Gentle Missing-Update Check-In) — daily cron, schedule check, mute check, single DM per person (Workflow 11)
- F-07 (Time-Logging Helper) — EOD draft dispatch (Workflow 12), Clockify delivery (Workflow 13), developer DM approval commands (Workflow 07 extension)
- New `docker-compose.yml` service: Whisper for in-house audio transcription (SC-10)
- New env vars: `CHECKIN_TIME`, `WHISPER_BASE_URL`

**Out (explicitly):**
- F-06 (Unblock Assistance) — beyond P1
- Any changes to P0 workflows 00–08 except the targeted extension to Workflow 07's message classifier
- Multi-agent orchestration (F-09) — WON'T
- Individual productivity scoring (F-10) — WON'T, also prohibited by SC-04
- Changes to `drafts` table structure — new `time_entry` type slots into existing schema without column changes
- Google Calendar write (calendar event creation) — that is F-06 scope

---

## Shared Infrastructure Additions

**All P0 shared conventions continue unchanged:**
- Postgres connection, Discord HTTP base, Ollama request shape, roster load helper, env var check, privacy name scan, mute check, idempotent write patterns — as specified in `specs/p0-workflow-plan.md`.

**New reusable helper — Schedule Check (used by Workflows 11 and 12):**

Every schedule determination queries Clockify and Google Calendar live (SC-18). The following logic is extracted as a named Code node pattern (not a sub-workflow — the state is local and async calls differ by developer):

```javascript
// Node name: "Check Scheduled Status — <developer_name>"
// Inputs: developer.clockify_id, developer.google_calendar_id, developer.discord_id
// Returns: { is_scheduled: boolean, source: 'clockify+calendar'|'clockify_only'|'calendar_only'|'both_unavailable' }

const today = new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10); // YYYY-MM-DD

// Clockify time-off endpoint (primary):
// GET https://api.clockify.me/api/v1/workspaces/{CLOCKIFY_WORKSPACE_ID}/time-off/requests
//   ?status=APPROVED&start={today}T00:00:00Z&end={today}T23:59:59Z
// Header: X-Api-Key: $env.CLOCKIFY_API_KEY
// on_leave_clockify = response items contain an entry covering developer.clockify_id for today

// Google Calendar freebusy (secondary):
// POST https://www.googleapis.com/calendar/v3/freeBusy
// Header: Authorization: Bearer <refreshed_google_access_token>
// Body: { "timeMin": "{today}T00:00:00Z", "timeMax": "{today}T23:59:59Z",
//         "items": [{ "id": developer.google_calendar_id }] }
// on_leave_calendar = response.calendars[developer.google_calendar_id].busy.length > 0

// Fail-safe (SC-18): if both unavailable → is_scheduled = false, source = 'both_unavailable'
// Stricter wins (SC-06a): if either source marks unavailable → is_scheduled = false
```

This pattern is embedded inline in Workflows 11 and 12. The exact HTTP Request nodes for Clockify and Google Calendar are specified within each workflow.

**Google token refresh helper (reused from P0 Workflow 4b pattern):**
- Node type: HTTP Request
- Method: POST `https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token`
- Body: `{ "client_id": "{{ $env.GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID }}", "client_secret": "{{ $env.GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET }}", "refresh_token": "{{ $env.GOOGLE_REFRESH_TOKEN }}", "grant_type": "refresh_token" }`
- Extract `access_token` from response for downstream calls.

**New `drafts.content_json` structure for `time_entry` type:**
```json
{
  "text": "<human-readable description of the draft, shown in DM>",
  "metadata": {
    "clockify_id": "<developer's clockify_id>",
    "developer_discord_id": "<discord_id>",
    "date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
    "description": "<proposed time entry description>",
    "start_iso": "<ISO 8601 datetime — today at 09:00 local, formatted as UTC>",
    "end_iso": "<ISO 8601 datetime — today at EOD_TIME local, formatted as UTC>"
  }
}
```

---

## Schema Additions (postgres/init.sql)

No new tables are needed. All P1 state uses the existing `project_memory` table and the existing `drafts` table.

**Add the following index to `init.sql` (idempotent `IF NOT EXISTS`):**
```sql
-- Speeds up draft lookup by developer discord_id for time_entry approval polling
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS drafts_type_approver_status_idx
  ON drafts (type, approver_discord_id, status);
```

**`project_picture` key format (defined here, consumed by Workflows 09, 10, and by P0 Workflows 04a, 05a, 07):**

The value stored at `project_memory` key `project_picture` must be a JSONB object with this structure:

```json
{
  "last_updated": "ISO 8601 timestamp",
  "data_sources": ["standup_2026-06-29", "github", "jira"],
  "features": [
    {
      "name": "Feature name",
      "status": "in_progress | blocked | completed | unknown",
      "recent_progress": "project-level sentence — no individual attribution",
      "blockers": "project-level sentence or null",
      "last_signal_date": "YYYY-MM-DD"
    }
  ],
  "open_blockers": ["project-level blocker description"],
  "standup_data_available": true,
  "bootstrap_note": null
}
```

When `standup_data_available` is `false` (day-1 bootstrap, F-14.6), `bootstrap_note` must be set to `"No standup data available yet. Initial picture derived from GitHub/Jira supplementary signals only."` The F-01 and F-02 prompts already read this field in P0 and pass it through to the LLM — the LLM system prompts for those workflows instruct the LLM to surface this note honestly in drafts.

---

## Workflow 09: Update Project Picture Sub-Workflow (F-14)

**Purpose:** Merge freshly parsed standup data into the stored `project_picture`. Called by Workflow 10 after each processed transcript. Stateless and re-entrant — safe to retry.

**Trigger:** Execute Workflow Trigger (sub-workflow). Receives input JSON from Workflow 10:

```json
{
  "standup_date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "parsed_updates": [
    {
      "area": "feature or work area name (project-level)",
      "status_hint": "progressing | blocked | completed | unknown",
      "progress_note": "project-level summary sentence — no individual names",
      "has_blocker": true,
      "blocker_note": "project-level blocker description or null"
    }
  ],
  "unattributed_notes": ["project-level notes that could not be assigned to a work area"],
  "roster_names": ["Alex", "Sam", ...]
}
```

**Node sequence:**

1. **Sub-workflow Input** — type: Execute Workflow Trigger. Receives the JSON above.

2. **Load Existing Project Picture** — type: Postgres.
   ```sql
   SELECT value_json FROM project_memory WHERE key = 'project_picture' LIMIT 1
   ```
   Returns the current `project_picture` object or null if not yet set.

3. **Assemble LLM Merge Prompt** — type: Code.

   Constructs the Ollama request body. Includes:
   - `existing_picture`: the current `project_picture` value_json, serialized as JSON string. If null, use the string `"No existing picture. This is the first ingestion."`.
   - `new_standup_data`: the `parsed_updates` array from the sub-workflow input, serialized as JSON string.
   - `unattributed_notes`: serialized array.
   - `standup_date`: the date string.

   **System prompt (exact text — do not alter):**
   ```
   You are a project coordination assistant maintaining a project picture. Your task is to merge a new standup summary into the existing project picture.

   MANDATORY RULES — violating any is a critical error:
   1. The project picture must contain ONLY project-level information. No individual names, roles, initials, or any detail that could identify a specific person may appear anywhere in the output.
   2. Do not include commit counts, hours logged, or any numeric metric attributable to an individual.
   3. The newer standup signal always takes precedence over older stored data for the same feature (F-14.5). Update accordingly.
   4. If the existing picture has features not mentioned in the new standup, preserve them but do not mark them as stale unless the standup explicitly says a feature is complete.
   5. Do not fabricate progress or blockers. If a feature's status is unclear from the new data, set its status to "unknown".
   6. Retain the bootstrap_note if standup_data_available was false — the new ingestion sets standup_data_available to true and clears bootstrap_note.
   7. Output ONLY valid JSON matching this schema exactly — no prose, no markdown fences, no commentary outside the JSON:
   {
     "last_updated": "<ISO 8601 timestamp — use the standup_date provided>",
     "data_sources": ["<list of strings identifying sources>"],
     "features": [
       {
         "name": "<feature name>",
         "status": "<in_progress|blocked|completed|unknown>",
         "recent_progress": "<one sentence, project-level>",
         "blockers": "<one sentence or null>",
         "last_signal_date": "<YYYY-MM-DD>"
       }
     ],
     "open_blockers": ["<project-level blocker string>"],
     "standup_data_available": true,
     "bootstrap_note": null
   }
   ```

   **User message:**
   ```
   Existing project picture: {{ existing_picture_json }}

   New standup data (date: {{ standup_date }}): {{ new_standup_data_json }}

   Unattributed notes from standup: {{ unattributed_notes_json }}

   Produce the updated project picture JSON.
   ```

4. **Call Ollama — Merge** — type: HTTP Request.
   - Method: POST `{{ $env.OLLAMA_BASE_URL }}/v1/chat/completions`
   - Body: `{ "model": "llama3", "messages": [{"role": "system", "content": "<system prompt>"}, {"role": "user", "content": "<user message>"}], "temperature": 0.1, "stream": false }`
   - Timeout: 90s
   - `continueOnFail: true`

5. **Parse and Validate LLM Output** — type: Code.

   ```javascript
   const response = $input.first().json;
   if (response.error || !response.choices) {
     // LLM call failed — return error signal, do not write to Postgres
     return [{ json: { success: false, reason: 'LLM unavailable: ' + JSON.stringify(response.error) } }];
   }
   let raw = response.choices[0].message.content.trim();
   // Strip any accidental markdown fences
   raw = raw.replace(/^```json\s*/i, '').replace(/\s*```$/, '');
   let parsed;
   try {
     parsed = JSON.parse(raw);
   } catch (e) {
     return [{ json: { success: false, reason: 'LLM output not valid JSON: ' + e.message } }];
   }
   // Minimal schema validation
   if (!parsed.features || !Array.isArray(parsed.features)) {
     return [{ json: { success: false, reason: 'LLM output missing features array' } }];
   }
   return [{ json: { success: true, picture: parsed } }];
   ```

6. **Privacy Validation on Picture** — type: Code.

   Load `roster_names` from the sub-workflow input. Scan the entire serialized picture string for any roster member name as a whole word (case-insensitive `\b<name>\b`). If any found: log a warning and replace each with `[REMOVED]`. This is a safety net — the LLM prompt is the primary control.

   ```javascript
   const rosterNames = $('Sub-workflow Input').first().json.roster_names || [];
   let pictureStr = JSON.stringify($json.picture);
   let warningAdded = false;
   for (const name of rosterNames) {
     const re = new RegExp('\\b' + name.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&') + '\\b', 'gi');
     if (re.test(pictureStr)) {
       pictureStr = pictureStr.replace(re, '[REMOVED]');
       warningAdded = true;
     }
   }
   const finalPicture = JSON.parse(pictureStr);
   return [{ json: { success: $json.success, picture: finalPicture, privacy_warning: warningAdded } }];
   ```

7. **Route: Success?** — type: IF.
   - Condition: `{{ $json.success === true }}`
   - True → step 8 (write to Postgres)
   - False → step 9 (log failure, return error)

8. **Upsert Project Picture** — type: Postgres.
   ```sql
   INSERT INTO project_memory (key, value_json)
   VALUES ('project_picture', $1::jsonb)
   ON CONFLICT (key) DO UPDATE
     SET value_json = EXCLUDED.value_json,
         updated_at = now()
   ```
   Query replacement: `={{ JSON.stringify($json.picture) }}`

   Then return `{ success: true }` as sub-workflow output.

9. **Return Error** — type: Code.
   Returns `{ success: false, reason: $json.reason }` as sub-workflow output. The caller (Workflow 10) logs this failure to the Team Lead channel.

**Privacy checkpoint (step 6):** Roster name scan applied to the entire merged picture before it is ever written to Postgres or returned.

**Idempotency:** The upsert in step 8 is a safe-to-retry operation. Repeated calls with the same standup data will overwrite with the same (or very similar) result. Since the LLM is non-deterministic at temperature 0.1, exact idempotency of content is not guaranteed, but the idempotency of the file-ID deduplication in Workflow 10 prevents the same transcript from being processed twice.

**Error handling:** If Ollama is unavailable (step 4 fails), returns `{ success: false, reason: "LLM unavailable" }` — Workflow 10 posts a notice to the Team Lead channel. The transcript is NOT marked as processed in `standup_processed_file_ids`, so the next run of Workflow 10 will retry it.

---

## Workflow 10: Standup Transcript Watcher (F-04)

**Purpose:** Polls the Google Drive watched folder for new standup transcript files. Parses text files directly. Sends audio files to the local Whisper service for transcription. Extracts project-level updates. Calls Workflow 09 to update the project picture. Writes standup-heard records for F-05.

**Trigger:** Schedule Trigger — `*/15 * * * *` (every 15 minutes).

Rationale for 15 minutes: transcripts are typically dropped manually after a standup meeting and do not require sub-minute latency. 15-minute polling keeps Drive API call volume low.

**Node sequence:**

1. **Cron Trigger** — type: Schedule Trigger, `cronExpression: */15 * * * *`

2. **Load Roster** — type: Code. Reads `/data/roster.yml` with standard helper. Extracts:
   - `roster.team` (active members: `discord_id`, `name`, `clockify_id`)
   - `roster.team_lead_channel_id`
   - `roster_names` = array of all member names + team_lead name (for fuzzy-match and privacy scan)

3. **Check Env Vars** — type: Code. Verifies `DRIVE_FOLDER_ID`, `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID`, `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET`, `GOOGLE_REFRESH_TOKEN`, `OLLAMA_BASE_URL` are non-empty. Throws if absent.

4. **Refresh Google Token** — type: HTTP Request. POST `https://oauth2.googleapis.com/token` with standard body. Extract `access_token`.

5. **Read Drive Poll Timestamp** — type: Postgres.
   ```sql
   SELECT value_json FROM project_memory WHERE key = 'standup_drive_last_check_timestamp' LIMIT 1
   ```
   If no row found, use timestamp `2000-01-01T00:00:00Z` (processes all files on first run — acceptable for initial bootstrap).

6. **Read Processed File IDs** — type: Postgres.
   ```sql
   SELECT value_json FROM project_memory WHERE key = 'standup_processed_file_ids' LIMIT 1
   ```
   If no row found, treat as empty array `[]`.

7. **Query Drive for New Files** — type: HTTP Request.
   - Method: GET
   - URL: `https://www.googleapis.com/drive/v3/files`
   - Query params:
     - `q`: `'{{ $env.DRIVE_FOLDER_ID }}' in parents and modifiedTime > '{{ last_check_timestamp }}' and trashed=false`
     - `fields`: `files(id,name,mimeType,modifiedTime,size)`
     - `orderBy`: `modifiedTime asc`
     - `pageSize`: `50`
   - Header: `Authorization: Bearer {{ access_token }}`
   - `continueOnFail: true`

8. **Update Drive Poll Timestamp** — type: Postgres. Upsert `standup_drive_last_check_timestamp` with `now()` as ISO string. This is written immediately after the Drive query so that even if processing fails below, the next run does not re-fetch the same file list. Files that fail processing are caught by the deduplication check separately.

   ```sql
   INSERT INTO project_memory (key, value_json)
   VALUES ('standup_drive_last_check_timestamp', $1::jsonb)
   ON CONFLICT (key) DO UPDATE SET value_json = EXCLUDED.value_json, updated_at = now()
   ```
   Query replacement: `={{ JSON.stringify(new Date().toISOString()) }}`

9. **Handle Drive API Failure** — type: IF. Condition: `{{ $json.error !== undefined OR $node['Query Drive for New Files'].json.files === undefined }}`.
   - True (error): Post notice to Team Lead channel: `"Standup watcher: Google Drive API unavailable. Will retry in 15 minutes."` Stop.
   - False: Continue.

10. **Filter to Unprocessed Files** — type: Code.

    ```javascript
    const driveResult = $('Query Drive for New Files').first().json;
    const processedIds = $('Read Processed File IDs').first().json?.value_json || [];
    const files = driveResult.files || [];

    const supported_text = ['text/plain', 'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document'];
    const supported_audio = ['audio/mpeg', 'audio/mp4', 'audio/wav', 'audio/x-wav', 'audio/x-m4a'];

    const unprocessed = files.filter(f => !processedIds.includes(f.id));

    return unprocessed.map(f => {
      let file_class = 'unsupported';
      if (supported_text.includes(f.mimeType) ||
          f.name.match(/\.(txt|docx)$/i)) file_class = 'text';
      else if (supported_audio.includes(f.mimeType) ||
               f.name.match(/\.(mp3|m4a|wav)$/i)) file_class = 'audio';
      return { json: { ...f, file_class } };
    });
    ```

    Note: If `unprocessed` is empty, `SplitInBatches` will immediately exhaust and the workflow ends gracefully.

11. **For Each File** — type: SplitInBatches, batch size 1.

12. **Route by File Class** — type: Switch. Field: `{{ $json.file_class }}`.
    - `text` → Branch T (steps T1–T3)
    - `audio` → Branch A (steps A1–A3)
    - `unsupported` → Branch U (step U1)

**Branch T — Text file:**

T1. **Download Text File Content** — type: HTTP Request.
   - Method: GET
   - URL: `https://www.googleapis.com/drive/v3/files/{{ $json.id }}?alt=media`
   - Header: `Authorization: Bearer {{ access_token }}`
   - Response format: text (raw body)
   - `continueOnFail: true`

   For `.docx` files: the raw binary will not be readable as plain text. The builder must use `alt=media` and set `responseFormat: 'file'` then convert with a Code node using the `mammoth` npm package (`mammoth.extractRawText({ buffer: Buffer.from(content, 'base64') })`). Mammoth is available in n8n's default Docker image. If mammoth extraction fails, treat as unsupported and route to U1.

   For `.txt` files: `alt=media` returns plain UTF-8 text directly.

   Decision: detect by `mimeType`. `text/plain` → raw text. `application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document` → mammoth extraction. Expose `raw_text` string from either path.

T2. **Parse Standup Text** — type: Code + HTTP Request. Call Ollama.

   **System prompt (exact text — do not alter):**
   ```
   You are a project coordination assistant parsing a standup transcript. Extract project-level progress updates.

   MANDATORY RULES:
   1. Do NOT attribute any update to a named individual. Convert all speaker-attributed content to work-area level.
      Example: "Alice said the auth feature is done" → { "area": "auth feature", "status_hint": "completed", "progress_note": "Authentication feature completed." }
   2. If you cannot determine which work area an update belongs to, place it in unattributed_notes as a project-level sentence.
   3. Do NOT fabricate content. Extract only what is explicitly stated.
   4. Do NOT include any person's name in any output field.
   5. Output ONLY valid JSON — no prose, no markdown fences:
   {
     "updates": [
       {
         "area": "<work area or feature name>",
         "status_hint": "<progressing|blocked|completed|unknown>",
         "progress_note": "<one sentence, project-level, no names>",
         "has_blocker": <true|false>,
         "blocker_note": "<project-level blocker description or null>"
       }
     ],
     "unattributed_notes": ["<project-level note>"],
     "parse_quality": "<good|partial|poor>"
   }
   If the transcript is garbled, partial, or unattributable (F-04.3), set parse_quality to "partial" or "poor" and extract only what is reliable. Do not fabricate.
   ```

   **User message:**
   ```
   Standup transcript (date: {{ today_date }}, file: {{ $json.name }}):

   {{ raw_text }}

   Extract project-level updates. No individual names in output.
   ```

   Ollama call: POST `{{ $env.OLLAMA_BASE_URL }}/v1/chat/completions`, `temperature: 0.1`, `stream: false`, timeout: 120s. `continueOnFail: true`.

T3. **Parse Standup LLM Response** — type: Code. Parse JSON from `choices[0].message.content`. Strip markdown fences. If parse fails or response is error: set `parse_quality = 'failed'` and `updates = []`, `unattributed_notes = ['Transcript parse failed — LLM error.']`. Continue to step 13 (do not halt; F-04.5 behavior for parse failures).

**Branch A — Audio file:**

A1. **Check Whisper Availability** — type: HTTP Request.
   - Method: GET `{{ $env.WHISPER_BASE_URL }}/healthz`
   - `continueOnFail: true`
   - Timeout: 10s

   If response is not 2xx or times out: post notice to Team Lead channel (`"Standup watcher: audio file '{{ $json.name }}' skipped — Whisper transcription service unavailable ({{ $env.WHISPER_BASE_URL }}/healthz). Resolve the service and re-drop the file or re-trigger manually."`). Mark file as processed (step 14) to prevent repeated failure notices on every poll. Set `parse_quality = 'whisper_unavailable'` and `updates = []`. Continue to step 14 directly.

A2. **Download Audio File** — type: HTTP Request.
   - Method: GET `https://www.googleapis.com/drive/v3/files/{{ $json.id }}?alt=media`
   - Header: `Authorization: Bearer {{ access_token }}`
   - Response format: binary / file
   - `continueOnFail: true`

A3. **Transcribe via Whisper** — type: HTTP Request.
   - Method: POST `{{ $env.WHISPER_BASE_URL }}/asr`
   - Content-Type: `multipart/form-data`
   - Form field `audio_file`: the downloaded binary
   - Form field `task`: `transcribe`
   - Form field `language`: `en`
   - Timeout: 300s (5 minutes — audio files can be large)
   - `continueOnFail: true`

   The Whisper service used is `onerahmet/openai-whisper-asr-webservice` (see docker-compose additions below). Its `/asr` endpoint accepts multipart form data and returns a JSON response with a `text` field containing the transcribed text.

   If response is not 2xx: post notice to Team Lead channel. Mark file as processed. Set `updates = []`, `parse_quality = 'transcription_failed'`. Continue to step 14 directly.

   On success: extract `response.text` as `raw_text`. Then execute steps T2 and T3 (the same Ollama parse nodes — connect Branch A3 output into the T2 node).

**Branch U — Unsupported format:**

U1. **Log Unsupported File** — type: HTTP Request. POST to Team Lead approval channel:
   ```json
   { "content": "Standup watcher: unsupported file format skipped — '{{ $json.name }}' (MIME: {{ $json.mimeType }}). Drop a .txt, .docx, .mp3, .m4a, or .wav file instead. (F-04.5)" }
   ```
   Then continue to step 14 to mark as processed so it is not re-flagged on every poll cycle.

**Rejoin point — all branches converge here:**

13. **Write Standup-Heard Records** — type: Code + Postgres.

    From the parsed output: identify which roster members were heard from. Match strategy: the LLM has already stripped names, but Workflow 10 has access to the original raw text (before LLM processing) solely for the purpose of matching speaker names to `discord_id` values for `standup_heard_{YYYY-MM-DD}`. This is the only place raw attribution data is used, and it never leaves this node.

    ```javascript
    // Fuzzy-match roster member names against the raw transcript text (before LLM stripping)
    // to build the list of who was heard from today.
    // This data is used exclusively for F-05 (check-in targeting) and is never surfaced upward.
    const rawText = $('T1/T2 path').json.raw_text || '';
    const rosterTeam = $('Load Roster').first().json.team || [];
    const today = new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10);
    const heardIds = [];

    for (const member of rosterTeam) {
      // Simple whole-word name match (case-insensitive)
      const firstName = member.name.split(' ')[0];
      const re = new RegExp('\\b' + firstName.replace(/[.*+?^${}()|[\]\\]/g, '\\$&') + '\\b', 'i');
      if (re.test(rawText)) {
        heardIds.push(member.discord_id);
      }
    }

    return [{ json: { today, heard_ids: heardIds } }];
    ```

    Then Postgres upsert:
    ```sql
    INSERT INTO project_memory (key, value_json)
    VALUES ('standup_heard_' || $1, $2::jsonb)
    ON CONFLICT (key) DO UPDATE
      SET value_json = (
        SELECT jsonb_agg(DISTINCT elem)
        FROM jsonb_array_elements_text(
          project_memory.value_json || EXCLUDED.value_json
        ) AS elem
      ),
      updated_at = now()
    ```
    Query replacements: `={{ $json.today }}`, `={{ JSON.stringify($json.heard_ids) }}`

    This upsert merges new `discord_id` values into the existing array for the day, so multiple transcript files on the same day accumulate rather than overwrite.

14. **Mark File as Processed** — type: Code + Postgres.

    ```javascript
    const fileId = $('For Each File').first().json.id;
    const processed = $('Read Processed File IDs').first().json?.value_json || [];
    if (!processed.includes(fileId)) processed.push(fileId);
    // Keep only the last 500 file IDs to prevent unbounded growth
    const trimmed = processed.slice(-500);
    return [{ json: { file_ids: trimmed } }];
    ```

    Postgres upsert:
    ```sql
    INSERT INTO project_memory (key, value_json)
    VALUES ('standup_processed_file_ids', $1::jsonb)
    ON CONFLICT (key) DO UPDATE SET value_json = EXCLUDED.value_json, updated_at = now()
    ```

15. **Route: Call Workflow 09?** — type: IF. Condition: `{{ $json.updates !== undefined AND $json.updates.length > 0 }}` (only call if there is something to merge).
    - True → step 16
    - False → return to SplitInBatches (step 11) for next file

16. **Call Workflow 09 — Update Project Picture** — type: Execute Workflow. Calls Workflow 09 with:
    ```json
    {
      "standup_date": "{{ today_date }}",
      "parsed_updates": "{{ parsed_updates_array }}",
      "unattributed_notes": "{{ unattributed_notes_array }}",
      "roster_names": "{{ roster_names_array }}"
    }
    ```

17. **Handle Workflow 09 Failure** — type: IF. Condition: `{{ $json.success === false }}`.
    - True: Post to Team Lead channel: `"Standup watcher: project picture update failed for file '{{ file_name }}'. Reason: {{ reason }}. The file has been marked as processed. Re-trigger Workflow 09 manually or re-process the transcript."` Continue.
    - False: Continue.

    After step 17, execution returns to SplitInBatches (step 11) for the next file.

**Privacy checkpoints:**
- Step T2 LLM system prompt: strips all individual attribution before the result is stored or returned.
- Step 13: raw text used only for roster name matching within the Code node; heard IDs (`discord_id` values) written to `project_memory` — not names, not content. The `standup_heard_*` keys are consumed only by Workflow 11 to determine DM targeting.
- The `raw_text` of the transcript is never written to Postgres. Only the LLM-processed, anonymised `parsed_updates` are forwarded to Workflow 09.

**Error handling summary:**
- Drive API unavailable (step 9): notice to Team Lead channel, stop.
- Unsupported file (step 12, Branch U): log to Team Lead channel, mark processed, continue.
- Whisper unavailable (step A1): log to Team Lead channel, mark processed, continue.
- Whisper transcription failed (step A3): log to Team Lead channel, mark processed, continue.
- LLM parse failed (step T3): `updates = []`, `parse_quality = 'failed'`; file marked processed; no project picture update attempted; continue.
- Workflow 09 returned failure (step 17): log to Team Lead channel, continue.

**Idempotency:**
- `standup_processed_file_ids` (step 14): prevents any file from being processed twice.
- `standup_drive_last_check_timestamp` (step 8): written before processing begins so retries do not re-query the same file set.

---

## Workflow 11: EOD Check-In Dispatcher (F-05)

**Purpose:** Daily cron at `CHECKIN_TIME`. For each active roster member: check if scheduled, check if heard from today, check if muted. If scheduled, not heard from, and not muted: send a single private, help-oriented DM. Idempotency key prevents re-sending on retry.

**Trigger:** Schedule Trigger — cron derived from `CHECKIN_TIME` env var. The n8n cron expression is `0 13 * * *` when `CHECKIN_TIME=13:00`. The builder must parse `CHECKIN_TIME` at deploy time and set the cron expression accordingly. If `CHECKIN_TIME` is absent, default to `0 13 * * *` (13:00 UTC). The Startup Validator (Workflow 00) must be extended to warn if `CHECKIN_TIME` is absent (non-fatal — it defaults).

**Node sequence:**

1. **Cron Trigger** — type: Schedule Trigger. `cronExpression: 0 13 * * *` (overridden at deploy from `CHECKIN_TIME`).

2. **Load Roster** — type: Code. Standard helper. Extracts active team members: `discord_id`, `name`, `clockify_id`, `google_calendar_id`. Extracts `team_lead_channel_id`.

3. **Check Env Vars** — type: Code. Verifies `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN`, `CLOCKIFY_API_KEY`, `CLOCKIFY_WORKSPACE_ID`, `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID`, `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET`, `GOOGLE_REFRESH_TOKEN` are non-empty. Non-fatal: logs warning if `CHECKIN_TIME` absent (defaulting to 13:00).

4. **Refresh Google Token** — type: HTTP Request. Standard pattern. Extract `access_token`.

5. **Read Today's Standup-Heard** — type: Postgres.
   ```sql
   SELECT value_json FROM project_memory
   WHERE key = 'standup_heard_' || to_char(now(), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
   LIMIT 1
   ```
   If no row: `heard_ids = []` (nobody heard from today — all scheduled members eligible for check-in; F-05.1 behavior).

6. **Expand Roster to Items** — type: Code. Returns one item per active team member with `heard_ids` attached.

7. **For Each Developer** — type: SplitInBatches, batch size 1.

8. **Check Already Heard From** — type: Code.
   ```javascript
   const heard = $('Read Today\'s Standup-Heard').first().json?.value_json || [];
   const discordId = $json.discord_id;
   const today = new Date().toISOString().slice(0, 10);
   return [{ json: { ...$json, already_heard: heard.includes(discordId), today } }];
   ```

9. **IF Already Heard From** — type: IF. Condition: `{{ $json.already_heard === true }}`.
   - True (already heard): skip this person — no check-in needed. Return to SplitInBatches.
   - False: continue to step 10.

10. **Check Idempotency — Already Sent Today** — type: Postgres.
    ```sql
    SELECT value_json FROM project_memory
    WHERE key = 'checkin_sent_' || $1 || '_' || $2
    LIMIT 1
    ```
    Query replacements: `={{ $json.today }}`, `={{ $json.discord_id }}`
    - If row found: check-in already sent today for this person — skip. Return to SplitInBatches.
    - If no row: continue to step 11.

11. **Check Mute List** — type: Postgres.
    ```sql
    SELECT discord_id FROM mute_list WHERE discord_id = $1
    ```
    Query replacement: `={{ $json.discord_id }}`
    - If row found (muted): skip. Return to SplitInBatches. (SC-12 / F-05.4)
    - If no row: continue to step 12.

12. **Check Clockify Schedule** — type: HTTP Request.
    - Method: GET
    - URL: `https://api.clockify.me/api/v1/workspaces/{{ $env.CLOCKIFY_WORKSPACE_ID }}/time-off/requests?status=APPROVED&start={{ today }}T00:00:00Z&end={{ today }}T23:59:59Z`
    - Header: `X-Api-Key: {{ $env.CLOCKIFY_API_KEY }}`
    - `continueOnFail: true`
    - Timeout: 15s

13. **Parse Clockify Result** — type: Code.
    ```javascript
    const result = $input.first().json;
    const clockifyId = $json.clockify_id;
    let clockify_available = true;
    let on_leave_clockify = false;

    if (result.error || !Array.isArray(result)) {
      clockify_available = false;
    } else {
      // Check if any approved time-off record covers this developer today
      on_leave_clockify = result.some(req =>
        req.userId === clockifyId && req.status === 'APPROVED'
      );
    }
    return [{ json: { ...$json, clockify_available, on_leave_clockify } }];
    ```

14. **Check Google Calendar OOO** — type: HTTP Request.
    - Method: POST `https://www.googleapis.com/calendar/v3/freeBusy`
    - Header: `Authorization: Bearer {{ access_token }}`
    - Body:
      ```json
      {
        "timeMin": "{{ today }}T00:00:00Z",
        "timeMax": "{{ today }}T23:59:59Z",
        "items": [{ "id": "{{ $json.google_calendar_id }}" }]
      }
      ```
    - `continueOnFail: true`
    - Timeout: 15s

15. **Evaluate Schedule Status** — type: Code.

    ```javascript
    const calResult = $input.first().json;
    const calId = $json.google_calendar_id;
    let calendar_available = true;
    let on_leave_calendar = false;

    if (calResult.error || !calResult.calendars) {
      calendar_available = false;
    } else {
      const cal = calResult.calendars[calId];
      on_leave_calendar = cal && cal.busy && cal.busy.length > 0;
    }

    const clockify_available = $json.clockify_available;
    const on_leave_clockify = $json.on_leave_clockify;

    let is_scheduled = true;
    let source = '';

    if (!clockify_available && !calendar_available) {
      // Both unavailable — fail safe, treat as not scheduled (SC-18 / F-05.5)
      is_scheduled = false;
      source = 'both_unavailable';
    } else if (!clockify_available) {
      // Calendar only
      is_scheduled = !on_leave_calendar;
      source = 'calendar_only';
    } else if (!calendar_available) {
      // Clockify only
      is_scheduled = !on_leave_clockify;
      source = 'clockify_only';
    } else {
      // Both available — stricter wins (SC-06a)
      is_scheduled = !on_leave_clockify && !on_leave_calendar;
      source = 'clockify+calendar';
    }

    return [{ json: { ...$json, is_scheduled, schedule_source: source } }];
    ```

16. **Handle Both Unavailable** — type: IF. Condition: `{{ $json.schedule_source === 'both_unavailable' }}`.
    - True: Post to Team Lead channel: `"Check-in dispatcher: could not determine schedule for one or more developers — both Clockify and Google Calendar unavailable. No check-ins sent today. (F-05.5 / SC-18)"` (post this message once, not per developer — use a flag or check if already posted this run). Return to SplitInBatches.
    - False: Continue.

    Implementation note: to avoid flooding the Team Lead channel with one notice per developer when both sources are down, use a `project_memory` key `checkin_sources_failed_{{ today }}`. If it exists and is true, skip posting again. If it does not exist, post the notice and upsert the key.

17. **IF Scheduled** — type: IF. Condition: `{{ $json.is_scheduled === true }}`.
    - False (not scheduled, SC-06 / F-05.2): skip. Return to SplitInBatches.
    - True: continue to step 18.

18. **Get or Open DM Channel** — type: HTTP Request.
    - Method: POST `https://discord.com/api/v10/users/@me/channels`
    - Header: `Authorization: Bot {{ $env.DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN }}`, `Content-Type: application/json`
    - Body: `{ "recipient_id": "{{ $json.discord_id }}" }`
    - `continueOnFail: true`

    Cache pattern: before this call, check `project_memory` key `dm_channel_{{ discord_id }}` (Postgres SELECT). If found, use cached value. If not found, make the POST and upsert the result.

19. **Send Check-In DM** — type: HTTP Request.
    - Method: POST `https://discord.com/api/v10/channels/{{ dm_channel_id }}/messages`
    - Header: `Authorization: Bot {{ $env.DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN }}`, `Content-Type: application/json`
    - Body (exact text — do not alter):
      ```json
      {
        "content": "Hey — we haven't heard from you in today's standup. No worries if things are busy! Is there anything you're working through that I can help coordinate? Feel free to ignore this if everything's on track."
      }
      ```
    - `continueOnFail: true`

    This message must never be altered to include: "You haven't logged", "You're behind", "Where's your update?", or any phrasing that implies demand or blame. The privacy rule here is directed downward (help-down), not aggregated upward. The message does not surface any individual data — it only acknowledges the person's absence from today's standup signal and offers help.

20. **Write Idempotency Key** — type: Postgres.
    ```sql
    INSERT INTO project_memory (key, value_json)
    VALUES ('checkin_sent_' || $1 || '_' || $2, $3::jsonb)
    ON CONFLICT (key) DO NOTHING
    ```
    Query replacements: `={{ $json.today }}`, `={{ $json.discord_id }}`, `={{ JSON.stringify({ sent_at: new Date().toISOString() }) }}`

    `ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING` is deliberate — if the DM send failed, we do NOT write the idempotency key so the next run retries. But if the DM send succeeded, write the key so the next run skips.

    Sequence: step 19 (send DM) must succeed before step 20 runs. Use an IF node after step 19 to check for `continueOnFail` errors before writing the key.

21. **IF DM Send Failed** — type: IF. After step 19. Condition: `{{ $json.error !== undefined }}`.
    - True: Log in n8n execution history. Do NOT write idempotency key. Return to SplitInBatches.
    - False: Go to step 20.

**Privacy checkpoints:**
- Step 8 / Check Already Heard From: `standup_heard_*` contains only `discord_id` arrays — no names, no content.
- Step 19 / DM text: the message contains no reference to any other person, no work content, no commit or hours data.
- No information is ever sent upward (to Team Lead channel) about which specific developer did or did not receive a check-in. The only Team Lead-facing message is the failure notice in step 16 (both sources unavailable), which is fully aggregate.

**Error handling:**
- Both scheduling sources unavailable: logged to Team Lead channel once; all developers skipped (F-05.5).
- DM channel unavailable: logged to n8n execution history only; idempotency key not written; retry next day.
- Muted developer: silently skipped (no log to Team Lead).

**Idempotency:**
- `checkin_sent_{{ today }}_{{ discord_id }}` key (step 20): written only after successful DM send. Prevents re-sending if workflow is re-triggered.

---

## Workflow 12: Time-Logging — EOD Draft and Dispatch (F-07 Draft)

**Purpose:** At EOD_TIME, for each scheduled developer who has not logged time today: draft a probable time entry and send it to their Discord DM via the approval gate (Workflow 02).

**Trigger:** Schedule Trigger — cron derived from `EOD_TIME` env var. When `EOD_TIME=17:00`, cron is `0 17 * * *`. Builder must parse `EOD_TIME` and set cron accordingly.

**Node sequence:**

1. **Cron Trigger** — type: Schedule Trigger. `cronExpression: 0 17 * * *` (overridden at deploy from `EOD_TIME`).

2. **Load Roster** — type: Code. Standard helper. Extracts active team members with `discord_id`, `name`, `clockify_id`, `google_calendar_id`. Extracts `team_lead_channel_id`.

3. **Check Env Vars** — type: Code. Verifies `EOD_TIME`, `CLOCKIFY_API_KEY`, `CLOCKIFY_WORKSPACE_ID`, `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN`, `OLLAMA_BASE_URL`, `GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID`, `GOOGLE_CLIENT_SECRET`, `GOOGLE_REFRESH_TOKEN`. Throws if absent.

4. **Refresh Google Token** — type: HTTP Request. Standard pattern. Extract `access_token`.

5. **Read Today's Standup-Heard** — type: Postgres.
   ```sql
   SELECT value_json FROM project_memory
   WHERE key = 'standup_heard_' || to_char(now(), 'YYYY-MM-DD')
   LIMIT 1
   ```
   If no row: `heard_ids = []`.

6. **Read Project Picture** — type: Postgres.
   ```sql
   SELECT value_json FROM project_memory WHERE key = 'project_picture' LIMIT 1
   ```
   If no row: `project_picture = null`.

7. **Expand Roster to Items** — type: Code. Returns one item per active team member with `heard_ids` and `project_picture` attached.

8. **For Each Developer** — type: SplitInBatches, batch size 1.

9. **Check Clockify Schedule** — type: HTTP Request. Same as Workflow 11 step 12.

10. **Parse Clockify Result** — type: Code. Same as Workflow 11 step 13.

11. **Check Google Calendar OOO** — type: HTTP Request. Same as Workflow 11 step 14.

12. **Evaluate Schedule Status** — type: Code. Same as Workflow 11 step 15. If `both_unavailable`: post notice to Team Lead channel (once per run, using `timelog_sources_failed_{{ today }}` key as flag) and skip all developers. (F-07.6 / SC-18)

13. **IF Scheduled** — type: IF. `{{ $json.is_scheduled === true }}`.
    - False: skip. Return to SplitInBatches.
    - True: continue.

14. **Check Clockify for Existing Time Entries** — type: HTTP Request.
    - Method: GET
    - URL: `https://api.clockify.me/api/v1/workspaces/{{ $env.CLOCKIFY_WORKSPACE_ID }}/user/{{ $json.clockify_id }}/time-entries?start={{ today }}T00:00:00Z&end={{ today }}T23:59:59Z&page=1&page-size=1`
    - Header: `X-Api-Key: {{ $env.CLOCKIFY_API_KEY }}`
    - `continueOnFail: true`
    - Timeout: 15s

15. **Evaluate Time Entry State** — type: Code.

    ```javascript
    const result = $input.first().json;
    let clockify_check_failed = false;
    let already_logged = false;

    if (result.error || !Array.isArray(result)) {
      clockify_check_failed = true;
    } else {
      already_logged = result.length > 0;
    }
    return [{ json: { ...$json, clockify_check_failed, already_logged } }];
    ```

16. **Route: Clockify Check Failed?** — type: IF. Condition: `{{ $json.clockify_check_failed === true }}`.
    - True: Get or open DM channel (standard cache pattern). Post DM to developer:
      ```
      Time-logging helper: I couldn't check your Clockify entries today — the service is unavailable. No time entry has been drafted. Please log your time manually when the service is back. (F-07.6)
      ```
      Return to SplitInBatches.
    - False: Continue.

17. **IF Already Logged** — type: IF. Condition: `{{ $json.already_logged === true }}`.
    - True (F-07.5): Get or open DM channel. Post DM to developer:
      ```
      Time-logging helper: You already have a time entry logged in Clockify for today. No action needed from me. Have a great rest of the day!
      ```
      Return to SplitInBatches.
    - False: Continue to step 18 (draft the entry).

18. **Check Draft Idempotency** — type: Postgres.
    ```sql
    SELECT id FROM drafts
    WHERE type = 'time_entry'
      AND approver_discord_id = $1
      AND status = 'pending'
      AND created_at > now() - INTERVAL '48 hours'
    LIMIT 1
    ```
    Query replacement: `={{ $json.discord_id }}`
    - If row found (pending draft already exists): skip. Return to SplitInBatches.
    - If no row: continue.

19. **Assemble Time Entry Draft Text** — type: Code + HTTP Request.

    Determine `eod_hour` and `eod_minute` from `$env.EOD_TIME` (split on `:`). Construct ISO datetimes:
    - `start_iso` = `{{ today }}T09:00:00Z` (hardcoded 9am start — future improvement: make configurable)
    - `end_iso` = `{{ today }}T{{ eod_hour }}:{{ eod_minute }}:00Z`

    Look up developer's `discord_id` in `standup_heard_{{ today }}` (already loaded in step 5). If they appear in the heard list, the standup data may contain clues about their work area.

    Assemble LLM prompt. Call Ollama.

    **System prompt (exact text — do not alter):**
    ```
    You are a project coordination assistant helping a developer log their time at the end of the day.

    MANDATORY RULES:
    1. Draft a short, honest time entry description (1-2 sentences) for the developer's day.
    2. Use only project-level feature context provided — do not invent tasks.
    3. Do not name any other team member.
    4. If no specific work context is available, produce a generic, honest placeholder. The developer will edit it.
    5. Phrase the description in first-person work terms, not in a status-report style.
    6. Output ONLY valid JSON — no prose, no fences:
       { "description": "<time entry description>" }
    ```

    **User message:**
    ```
    Developer work area context (project-level only): {{ standup_context_or_generic }}
    Today's date: {{ today }}
    Draft a probable time entry description for this developer's day.
    ```

    Where `standup_context_or_generic` is: if the developer was in `standup_heard_{{ today }}`, extract the relevant `features[].recent_progress` sentences from `project_picture` that match the standup date. If no standup context available: use `"No standup data available for today. Generic placeholder needed."`.

    Ollama call: `temperature: 0.2`, timeout: 60s, `continueOnFail: true`. If LLM fails: use fallback description `"Time entry for {{ today }} — please edit to reflect your actual work."`.

20. **Build Draft Content** — type: Code.

    ```javascript
    const llmResponse = $input.first().json;
    let description = 'Time entry for ' + $json.today + ' — please edit to reflect your actual work.';
    if (llmResponse.choices && llmResponse.choices[0]) {
      try {
        const parsed = JSON.parse(llmResponse.choices[0].message.content.trim().replace(/^```json\s*/i, '').replace(/\s*```$/, ''));
        if (parsed.description) description = parsed.description;
      } catch (e) { /* use fallback */ }
    }

    const today = $json.today;
    const eodParts = (process.env.EOD_TIME || '17:00').split(':');
    const eodHour = eodParts[0].padStart(2, '0');
    const eodMin = (eodParts[1] || '00').padStart(2, '0');

    const draftText = [
      `**Time Entry Draft — ${today}**`,
      '',
      `**Description:** ${description}`,
      `**Start:** ${today} 09:00 UTC`,
      `**End:** ${today} ${eodHour}:${eodMin} UTC`,
      '',
      'Reply `!approve <draft_id>` to log this entry, `!edit <draft_id> <new description>` to change the description, or `!reject <draft_id>` to skip.'
    ].join('\n');

    return [{
      json: {
        ...$json,
        description,
        start_iso: today + 'T09:00:00Z',
        end_iso: today + 'T' + eodHour + ':' + eodMin + ':00Z',
        draft_text: draftText
      }
    }];
    ```

21. **Call Workflow 02 — Post Draft** — type: Execute Workflow. Calls Workflow 02 with:
    ```json
    {
      "type": "time_entry",
      "content_text": "{{ draft_text }}",
      "approver_discord_id": "{{ discord_id }}",
      "approval_channel_id": "{{ dm_channel_id }}",
      "metadata_json": {
        "clockify_id": "{{ clockify_id }}",
        "developer_discord_id": "{{ discord_id }}",
        "date": "{{ today }}",
        "description": "{{ description }}",
        "start_iso": "{{ start_iso }}",
        "end_iso": "{{ end_iso }}"
      }
    }
    ```

    Note: Workflow 02 posts to `approval_channel_id`. For `time_entry` type, `approval_channel_id` is the developer's DM channel ID (not the Team Lead channel). This follows F-03.2: individual drafts go to that person's DM.

    The DM channel ID is obtained using the standard cache pattern (check `project_memory` key `dm_channel_{{ discord_id }}`, open DM if not cached) before calling Workflow 02.

    Workflow 02 checks idempotency by `type + approver_discord_id + status='pending'` — this matches the check in step 18, so the combined guarantee is: no duplicate pending `time_entry` drafts for the same developer.

22. **Log Action** — type: Postgres. Upsert `project_memory` key `action_log_timelog_draft_{{ discord_id }}_{{ today }}` with `{ "action": "time_entry_drafted", "draft_id": "{{ draft_id from WF02 output }}", "developer_discord_id": "{{ discord_id }}", "date": "{{ today }}", "timestamp": "{{ now }}" }`.

**Privacy checkpoints:**
- Step 19 LLM prompt: only project-level feature context (from `project_picture.features[].recent_progress`) passed to LLM — never raw standup text, never other developer names.
- The `draft_text` shown in the developer's DM contains only that developer's own proposed time entry. No other developer's data appears.
- The Team Lead channel receives no notification of time-entry drafts — only the developer sees their own draft (F-03.2).

**Error handling:**
- Both scheduling sources unavailable: notice to Team Lead channel once; skip all developers.
- Clockify time-entry check unavailable: DM the developer explaining the failure (F-07.6); no draft created.
- Ollama unavailable for draft text: use generic fallback description; draft is still created and sent.

**Idempotency:**
- Step 18: Postgres check for pending `time_entry` draft.
- Workflow 02 idempotency check: belt-and-suspenders second guard.

---

## Workflow 13: Time-Logging — Deliver to Clockify (F-07 Delivery)

**Purpose:** Called when a `time_entry` draft is approved (via Workflow 07 extension). Writes the approved time entry to Clockify. Idempotency check prevents duplicate entries.

**Trigger:** Execute Workflow Trigger. Inputs:
```json
{
  "draft_id": "<uuid>",
  "content_text": "<approved draft text — for reference only>",
  "metadata": {
    "clockify_id": "<developer's clockify_id>",
    "developer_discord_id": "<discord_id>",
    "date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
    "description": "<approved description>",
    "start_iso": "<ISO datetime>",
    "end_iso": "<ISO datetime>"
  }
}
```

**Node sequence:**

1. **Execute Workflow Trigger**

2. **Check Draft Already Executed** — type: Postgres.
   ```sql
   SELECT status FROM drafts WHERE id = $1
   ```
   Query replacement: `={{ $json.draft_id }}`
   - If `status = 'executed'`: post to developer DM: `"Time entry already logged. (Draft ID: {{ draft_id }})"`. Stop.
   - Otherwise: continue.

3. **Clockify Idempotency Check** — type: HTTP Request.
   - Method: GET
   - URL: `https://api.clockify.me/api/v1/workspaces/{{ $env.CLOCKIFY_WORKSPACE_ID }}/user/{{ $json.metadata.clockify_id }}/time-entries?start={{ metadata.date }}T00:00:00Z&end={{ metadata.date }}T23:59:59Z&page=1&page-size=5`
   - Header: `X-Api-Key: {{ $env.CLOCKIFY_API_KEY }}`
   - `continueOnFail: true`

4. **Evaluate Clockify Idempotency** — type: IF. Condition: `{{ Array.isArray($json) AND $json.length > 0 }}`.
   - True (entry already exists): Mark draft executed (step 7). Post to developer DM: `"Time entry for {{ metadata.date }} already exists in Clockify. Nothing was logged to avoid duplicates. (Draft ID: {{ draft_id }})"`. Stop.
   - False (no entry yet): Continue.
   - Error path (Clockify unavailable): Post to developer DM: `"Time-logging: Clockify is unavailable. Your approved entry has not been logged yet. The draft remains approved — I'll retry on the next run, or you can re-approve once Clockify is back. (Draft ID: {{ draft_id }})"`. Do NOT mark draft executed. Stop. (Safe retry: draft remains `approved`, Workflow 13 can be re-triggered.)

5. **Write Time Entry to Clockify** — type: HTTP Request.
   - Method: POST
   - URL: `https://api.clockify.me/api/v1/workspaces/{{ $env.CLOCKIFY_WORKSPACE_ID }}/time-entries`
   - Header: `X-Api-Key: {{ $env.CLOCKIFY_API_KEY }}`, `Content-Type: application/json`
   - Body:
     ```json
     {
       "description": "{{ metadata.description }}",
       "start": "{{ metadata.start_iso }}",
       "end": "{{ metadata.end_iso }}",
       "billable": true
     }
     ```
   - `continueOnFail: true`

6. **Handle Clockify Write Failure** — type: IF. Condition: `{{ $json.error !== undefined OR $json.id === undefined }}`.
   - True (write failed): Get or open developer DM channel. Post DM: `"Time-logging: failed to write time entry to Clockify. Error: {{ error_message }}. Your draft is still approved — please log manually or re-trigger. (Draft ID: {{ draft_id }})"`. Do NOT mark draft executed. Stop.
   - False: Continue.

7. **Mark Draft Executed** — type: Postgres.
   ```sql
   UPDATE drafts SET status = 'executed' WHERE id = $1 AND status = 'approved'
   ```
   Query replacement: `={{ $json.draft_id }}`
   (Conditional `AND status = 'approved'` guards against race conditions — F-03.4)

8. **Post Delivery Confirmation to Developer DM** — type: HTTP Request.
   - Get or open DM channel (cached). POST to DM:
     ```json
     { "content": "Done! Your time entry for {{ metadata.date }} has been logged to Clockify. Description: '{{ metadata.description }}'. (Draft ID: {{ draft_id }})" }
     ```

9. **Log Action** — type: Postgres. Upsert `project_memory` key `action_log_timelog_deliver_{{ draft_id }}` with `{ "action": "time_entry_logged", "draft_id": "...", "clockify_id": "...", "date": "...", "timestamp": "..." }`.

**Privacy checkpoints:**
- The only Discord surface is the developer's own DM — no Team Lead notification of individual time entry delivery.
- The `action_log` key does not store the time entry description content — only the clockify_id, date, and draft_id.

**Error handling:**
- Clockify read unavailable (step 3): notify developer DM, leave draft as `approved` for retry.
- Clockify write fails (step 5-6): notify developer DM, leave draft as `approved` for retry.
- Double-execution (step 7): `AND status = 'approved'` ensures 0 rows updated on second attempt.

**Idempotency:**
- Step 3: Clockify read check before write.
- Step 7: conditional `UPDATE WHERE status = 'approved'`.
- Step 2: draft status check.

---

## Workflow 07 Extension: Developer DM Listener — !approve / !reject / !edit for Time Entries

**Purpose:** Extend the existing Workflow 07 message classifier (step 9, `Detect Message Type` node) to recognize `!approve`, `!reject`, and `!edit` commands for `time_entry` drafts. These commands arrive in developer DMs. The extension routes approval commands to Workflow 02 execution and Workflow 13 delivery.

**Scope of change:** Add four new branches to the existing Switch node in Workflow 07 step 9. All other Workflow 07 nodes are unchanged.

**Updated message classifier (Code node — replace the body of `Detect Message Type`):**

```javascript
const content = ($json.message_content || '').trim();
const lower = content.toLowerCase();
let type;
if (lower === '!mute') {
  type = 'mute';
} else if (lower === '!unmute') {
  type = 'unmute';
} else if (lower.startsWith('!explain')) {
  type = 'explain';
} else if (lower.startsWith('!approve ')) {
  type = 'approve';
} else if (lower.startsWith('!reject ')) {
  type = 'reject';
} else if (lower.startsWith('!edit ')) {
  type = 'edit';
} else {
  type = 'qa';
}
return [{ json: { ...$json, message_type: type } }];
```

**Updated Switch node:** Add cases for `approve`, `reject`, `edit`. Existing `mute`, `unmute`, `explain`, `qa` cases unchanged.

**Branch: !approve `<draft_id>`**

ApprDev1. **Parse Draft ID** — type: Code.
```javascript
const parts = $json.message_content.trim().split(/\s+/);
const draftId = parts[1];
if (!draftId || !/^[0-9a-f-]{36}$/.test(draftId)) {
  return [{ json: { ...$json, parse_error: 'Invalid or missing draft ID. Usage: !approve <draft_id>' } }];
}
return [{ json: { ...$json, draft_id: draftId, parse_error: null } }];
```

ApprDev2. **IF Parse Error** — type: IF. `{{ $json.parse_error !== null }}`. True: reply to developer DM with parse error. Stop.

ApprDev3. **Fetch Draft** — type: Postgres.
```sql
SELECT id, type, content_json, status, approver_discord_id, expires_at
FROM drafts WHERE id = $1
```
Query replacement: `={{ $json.draft_id }}`

ApprDev4. **Validate Draft** — type: Code.
```javascript
const draft = $input.first().json;
const devId = $json.developer_discord_id;
if (!draft || !draft.id) return [{ json: { ...$json, valid: false, reason: 'Draft not found.' } }];
if (draft.status !== 'pending') return [{ json: { ...$json, valid: false, reason: 'Draft is not pending (status: ' + draft.status + ').' } }];
if (draft.approver_discord_id !== devId) return [{ json: { ...$json, valid: false, reason: 'You are not the approver for this draft.' } }];
if (new Date(draft.expires_at) < new Date()) return [{ json: { ...$json, valid: false, reason: 'Draft has expired.' } }];
if (draft.type !== 'time_entry') return [{ json: { ...$json, valid: false, reason: 'This command only works for time entry drafts.' } }];
return [{ json: { ...$json, valid: true, draft_type: draft.type, content_json: draft.content_json } }];
```

ApprDev5. **IF Not Valid** — type: IF. `{{ $json.valid === false }}`. True: reply to DM: `"{{ reason }}"`. Stop.

ApprDev6. **Mark Approved** — type: Postgres.
```sql
UPDATE drafts SET status = 'approved' WHERE id = $1 AND status = 'pending'
RETURNING id, type, content_json
```
Query replacement: `={{ $json.draft_id }}`
- If 0 rows returned: reply "Draft already processed." Stop.

ApprDev7. **Call Workflow 13 — Deliver** — type: Execute Workflow. Calls Workflow 13 with:
```json
{
  "draft_id": "{{ draft_id }}",
  "content_text": "{{ content_json.text }}",
  "metadata": "{{ content_json.metadata }}"
}
```

**Branch: !reject `<draft_id>`**

RejDev1–RejDev3. Same parse, fetch, validate pattern as ApprDev1–ApprDev4 (parse → validate). Validation: must be `pending`, correct `approver_discord_id`, not expired, type `time_entry`.

RejDev4. **Mark Rejected** — type: Postgres.
```sql
UPDATE drafts SET status = 'rejected' WHERE id = $1 AND status = 'pending'
```
If 0 rows: reply "Draft already processed." Stop.

RejDev5. **Post Rejection Confirmation** — type: HTTP Request. POST to developer DM: `"Time entry draft {{ draft_id }} rejected. Nothing has been logged to Clockify."`.

**Branch: !edit `<draft_id>` `<revised description>`**

EditDev1. **Parse** — type: Code.
```javascript
const content = $json.message_content.trim();
const parts = content.split(/\s+/);
const draftId = parts[1];
const revisedText = parts.slice(2).join(' ').trim();
if (!draftId || !/^[0-9a-f-]{36}$/.test(draftId)) {
  return [{ json: { ...$json, parse_error: 'Missing or invalid draft ID. Usage: !edit <draft_id> <new description>' } }];
}
if (!revisedText) {
  return [{ json: { ...$json, parse_error: 'Missing revised description. Usage: !edit <draft_id> <new description>' } }];
}
return [{ json: { ...$json, draft_id: draftId, revised_description: revisedText, parse_error: null } }];
```

EditDev2. **IF Parse Error** — reply to DM with error. Stop.

EditDev3–EditDev4. Fetch and validate draft (same as ApprDev3–ApprDev4).

EditDev5. **Update Draft Content and Mark Approved** — type: Postgres.
```sql
UPDATE drafts
SET content_json = jsonb_set(
      jsonb_set(content_json, '{text}', $2::jsonb),
      '{metadata,description}', $3::jsonb
    ),
    status = 'approved'
WHERE id = $1 AND status = 'pending'
RETURNING id, content_json
```
Query replacements:
- `$1` = `={{ $json.draft_id }}`
- `$2` = revised full `content_text` string (rebuild with new description) — set to `={{ JSON.stringify('**Time Entry Draft — ' + $json.today + '**\n\n**Description:** ' + $json.revised_description + '\n...') }}` — for simplicity, builder may pass only the description field: `JSON.stringify($json.revised_description)`
- `$3` = `={{ JSON.stringify($json.revised_description) }}`

EditDev6. **Call Workflow 13 — Deliver** — same as ApprDev7, reading updated `content_json.metadata` from the RETURNING row.

**Privacy checkpoint for all three branches:** The validation in ApprDev4/RejDev/EditDev checks `approver_discord_id === developer_discord_id` — a developer cannot approve, reject, or edit another developer's draft. This enforces F-03.2 at the application layer.

## Data Model Additions

**New `project_memory` keys for P1:**

| Key | Value Format | Written by | Read by |
|---|---|---|---|
| `standup_drive_last_check_timestamp` | `"ISO 8601 string"` | WF10 step 8 | WF10 step 5 |
| `standup_processed_file_ids` | `["drive_file_id", ...]` (max 500) | WF10 step 14 | WF10 step 6 |
| `standup_heard_YYYY-MM-DD` | `["discord_id", ...]` | WF10 step 13 | WF11 step 5, WF12 step 5 |
| `checkin_sent_YYYY-MM-DD_discord_id` | `{ "sent_at": "ISO" }` | WF11 step 20 | WF11 step 10 |
| `checkin_sources_failed_YYYY-MM-DD` | `{ "logged": true }` | WF11 step 16 | WF11 step 16 |
| `timelog_sources_failed_YYYY-MM-DD` | `{ "logged": true }` | WF12 step 12 | WF12 step 12 |
| `action_log_timelog_draft_discord_id_YYYY-MM-DD` | `{ action, draft_id, developer_discord_id, date, timestamp }` | WF12 step 22 | WF03 branch F (`!explain`) |
| `action_log_timelog_deliver_draft_id` | `{ action, draft_id, clockify_id, date, timestamp }` | WF13 step 9 | WF03 branch F (`!explain`) |

**`project_memory` key `project_picture`** — written by WF09, read by WF04a, WF05a, WF07, WF12. Format defined in "Schema Additions" section above.

**No new Postgres tables.** All P1 state uses `project_memory` and `drafts`.

**SQL addition to `postgres/init.sql`:**
```sql
-- Speeds up draft approval lookup by developer (time_entry type)
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS drafts_type_approver_status_idx
  ON drafts (type, approver_discord_id, status);
```

**roster.yml schema:** No changes needed. The `google_calendar_id` field already exists in `roster.example.yml`. Builder must confirm all team members have this field populated in the real `roster.yml` before deploying Workflows 11 and 12.

## Docker-Compose Additions

Add the following service to `docker-compose.yml` under the `services` block:

```yaml
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  # Whisper ASR — self-hosted audio transcription (SC-10)
  # Required for standup audio file ingestion (F-04.2).
  # SC-10: Must remain self-hosted; never replace with a cloud transcription API.
  # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
  whisper:
    image: onerahmet/openai-whisper-asr-webservice:latest
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      ASR_MODEL: base.en
      ASR_ENGINE: openai_whisper
    ports:
      - "9000:9000"
    networks:
      - pipemind
```

Notes:
- Model `base.en` is chosen for speed and low memory footprint on typical dev hardware. For production with longer audio, change to `small.en` or `medium.en` at the cost of higher RAM and slower transcription.
- The image exposes `/asr` (POST, multipart) and `/healthz` (GET). Workflow 10 uses both.
- Add `WHISPER_BASE_URL: http://whisper:9000` to the n8n service environment block in `docker-compose.yml`.
- No GPU required for `base.en`. CPU inference is adequate for standup-length audio (5–30 minutes).

**Add to the n8n `environment` block:**
```yaml
      CHECKIN_TIME: ${CHECKIN_TIME}
      WHISPER_BASE_URL: http://whisper:9000
```

**Add to `.env` (1Password references):**
```
CHECKIN_TIME=op://CoordinationAgent/config/checkin_time
WHISPER_BASE_URL=op://CoordinationAgent/config/whisper_base_url
```

**Update `volumes` block** — Whisper requires no persistent volume (models download to the container; acceptable for dev; for production, add a named volume to persist the downloaded ASR model):
```yaml
  whisper_models:
    driver: local
```
And in the whisper service:
```yaml
    volumes:
      - whisper_models:/root/.cache/whisper
```

## Startup Validator Extension (Workflow 00)

Add the following to Workflow 00's "Check Env Vars" node (step 2):

- `CHECKIN_TIME` — warn if absent (non-fatal, defaults to 13:00). Log warning to Team Lead channel: `"CHECKIN_TIME not set — defaulting to 13:00 UTC for check-in cron."`
- `WHISPER_BASE_URL` — warn if absent (non-fatal). Log warning: `"WHISPER_BASE_URL not set — audio standup transcription (F-04.2) will fail. Set to http://whisper:9000 if the Whisper container is running."`

These are non-fatal warnings because:
- `CHECKIN_TIME` has a safe default.
- `WHISPER_BASE_URL` absence only affects audio files; text transcripts still work.

## Cross-Workflow Dependencies

| Workflow | Depends on | Called by |
|---|---|---|
| WF09 (Update Project Picture) | Postgres `project_memory` | WF10 |
| WF10 (Standup Watcher) | Google Drive API, Whisper, Ollama, WF09 | none (cron) |
| WF11 (Check-In Dispatcher) | Clockify, Google Calendar, Postgres `project_memory`, `mute_list` | none (cron) |
| WF12 (EOD Draft) | Clockify, Google Calendar, Postgres, WF02 | none (cron) |
| WF13 (Clockify Delivery) | Clockify API, Postgres | WF07 extension |
| WF07 extension | Postgres `drafts`, WF13 | none (existing cron) |
| WF02 (Post Draft sub-WF) | Postgres `drafts` | WF12 |
| WF01 (Expiry Sweeper) | Postgres `drafts` | none (existing) — now also expires `time_entry` drafts |

**WF01 update note:** The existing Expiry Sweeper (Workflow 01) step 3 ("Notify Each Approver") must handle `time_entry` draft type. It currently routes `time_entry` to DM (as a developer draft). Confirm the existing condition `developer draft types ('time_entry') → DM to approver_discord_id` is already in the Workflow 01 implementation. If not, add `time_entry` to the DM routing branch in step 3.

**WF03 update note:** The existing Team Lead Listener (Workflow 03) Branch G ("Unrecognized") does not need changes — `!approve`, `!reject`, `!edit` are handled in the DM channel (Workflow 07 extension), not the Team Lead channel. If a team lead mistakenly types `!approve <id>` in the Team Lead channel, Branch G fires and replies "Unrecognized command" — this is correct behavior since `time_entry` draft approvals are DM-only.

## Privacy Checkpoints Summary

| Workflow | Step | Enforcement |
|---|---|---|
| WF09 — Update Project Picture | Step 3 (LLM System Prompt) | Prohibits individual names in project picture (primary control) |
| WF09 — Update Project Picture | Step 6 (Privacy Validation) | Post-LLM roster name scan on full picture JSON before write |
| WF10 — Standup Watcher | Step T2 (LLM System Prompt) | Strips speaker attribution; converts to work-area level |
| WF10 — Standup Watcher | Step 13 (Standup-Heard Write) | Only `discord_id` arrays written — no names, no content |
| WF10 — Standup Watcher | Raw text | `raw_text` used only in Code node for roster name matching; never written to Postgres |
| WF11 — Check-In Dispatcher | Step 19 (DM text) | Message contains no data about any other person; no work content |
| WF11 — Check-In Dispatcher | Step 16 (Team Lead notice) | Failure notice is fully aggregate — no named individual |
| WF12 — EOD Draft | Step 19 (LLM Prompt) | Only project-level `features[].recent_progress` from picture used — no raw standup |
| WF12 — EOD Draft | DM recipient | `time_entry` drafts go to developer DM only — never to Team Lead channel |
| WF13 — Clockify Delivery | Step 9 (Action Log) | Log stores only `clockify_id`, `date`, `draft_id` — not description content |
| WF07 extension | ApprDev4 (Validate) | `approver_discord_id === developer_discord_id` enforced — cross-developer approval blocked |
| All upward-facing content | N/A | `standup_heard_*` and `checkin_sent_*` keys are never exposed to Team Lead or Client surfaces |

## Test Scenarios

**F-14.1 (Record and retrieve project picture):**
Drop a text standup transcript in the Drive folder → Workflow 10 fires within 15 minutes → confirm `project_memory` row with key `project_picture` created/updated → confirm value contains `features` array with at least one entry → confirm no roster member names appear in value.

**F-14.2 (Individual-derived state stays behind aggregation boundary):**
After WF09 runs → read `project_picture` from Postgres → confirm `features[*].recent_progress` contains no roster member names → run WF04a (report draft) → confirm report draft contains no roster member names.

**F-14.4 (Inactive member historical data retained):**
Set a roster member to `active: false` in roster.yml → restart stack → run WF10 → confirm WF11 and WF12 do not contact that member → confirm their prior `standup_heard_*` entries remain in `project_memory` (not deleted).

**F-14.5 (Newer signal takes precedence):**
Insert a `project_picture` with `features[0].status = 'blocked'` for feature X → drop a standup transcript mentioning feature X as completed → run WF10 → confirm updated `project_picture` shows feature X as `completed`.

**F-14.6 (Day-1 bootstrapping):**
Delete `project_picture` key from `project_memory` → run WF04a (report draft) → confirm draft text includes the phrase referencing no standup data available (pulled from `bootstrap_note: null` absent case — the LLM prompt in WF04a already handles `project_picture = null` by noting "No stored project context yet.").

**F-04.1 (Text transcript ingestion — happy path):**
Drop a `.txt` file in DRIVE_FOLDER_ID → wait ≤15 minutes → confirm `standup_processed_file_ids` contains the file ID → confirm `project_picture` updated → confirm `standup_heard_{{ today }}` contains discord IDs of members mentioned by first name in the transcript.

**F-04.2 (Audio transcript ingestion):**
Drop an `.mp3` file → confirm Whisper `/asr` called → confirm transcribed text fed to Ollama → confirm `project_picture` updated.

**F-04.3 (Garbled transcript):**
Drop a `.txt` file containing only random characters → confirm LLM returns `parse_quality: poor` → confirm `updates = []` or `unattributed_notes` only → confirm no fabricated content in `project_picture`.

**F-04.4 (Duplicate file deduplication):**
Drop a `.txt` file → confirm processing → drop the same file again (same Drive file ID) → confirm `standup_processed_file_ids` check skips it → confirm `project_picture` not double-updated.

**F-04.5 (Unsupported format):**
Drop a `.pdf` file in the Drive folder → confirm Team Lead channel receives unsupported-format notice → confirm file added to `standup_processed_file_ids` → confirm workflow continues (does not halt).

**F-05.1 (Check-in sent to scheduled developer not heard from):**
Ensure developer is scheduled in Clockify and Calendar, and `standup_heard_{{ today }}` does NOT contain their discord_id → trigger WF11 → confirm single DM sent to developer's DM channel → confirm `checkin_sent_{{ today }}_{{ discord_id }}` key exists in `project_memory`.

**F-05.2 (Person not scheduled — no contact):**
Set developer as on leave in Clockify → run WF11 → confirm no DM sent to that developer.

**F-05.4 (Muted developer):**
Insert developer's `discord_id` into `mute_list` → run WF11 → confirm no DM sent.

**F-05.5 (Both scheduling sources unavailable):**
Mock Clockify and Calendar to return errors → run WF11 → confirm no DMs sent → confirm Team Lead channel receives one failure notice → confirm `checkin_sources_failed_{{ today }}` key written.

**F-07.1 (EOD draft — happy path):**
Scheduled developer with no Clockify time entry today → trigger WF12 → confirm `time_entry` draft created in `drafts` table → confirm developer DM contains draft with `!approve`, `!edit`, `!reject` instructions.

**F-07.2 (Developer approves time entry):**
Developer replies `!approve <draft_id>` in DM → confirm Workflow 07 extension routes to ApprDev branch → confirm draft status set to `approved` → confirm WF13 called → confirm time entry `POST` to Clockify → confirm draft status `executed` → confirm developer DM confirmation received.

**F-07.3 (Developer edits then approves):**
Developer replies `!edit <draft_id> worked on payments feature integration` → confirm `content_json.metadata.description` updated to new text → confirm WF13 called with updated description → confirm Clockify entry uses the edited description.

**F-07.3 (Developer rejects):**
Developer replies `!reject <draft_id>` → confirm draft status `rejected` → confirm no Clockify write → confirm DM confirmation.

**F-07.4 (Not scheduled — no draft):**
Mark developer as on leave → trigger WF12 → confirm no draft created for that developer.

**F-07.5 (Already logged):**
Insert a Clockify time entry for today for the developer (via direct API) → trigger WF12 → confirm no draft created → confirm developer DM receives "already logged" notice.

**F-07.6 (Clockify unavailable at EOD):**
Mock Clockify to return 500 on time-entry check → trigger WF12 → confirm developer DM receives failure notice → confirm no draft created → confirm workflow does not crash.

**F-03.4 (Duplicate approval):**
Approve a `time_entry` draft → immediately reply `!approve <same_draft_id>` → confirm second `UPDATE WHERE status = 'pending'` returns 0 rows → confirm WF13 not called a second time.

**SC-21 (Clockify delivery idempotency):**
Approve draft → Clockify write succeeds → mark executed → trigger WF13 again with same `draft_id` → confirm step 2 ("Draft Already Executed") fires → no second Clockify POST.

**SC-06 (No contact when not scheduled):**
Run WF11 and WF12 with all developers marked as on leave in both Clockify and Calendar → confirm zero DMs sent, zero drafts created.

**Privacy — names in project picture:**
After WF09 runs: read `project_picture.value_json` from Postgres → assert that no string matching any roster member's name appears anywhere in the JSON document.
